name: DJI Mini 4 Pro Drone

specs.Weight: 249g (sub-250g — Transport Canada micro RPAS, no registration required)
specs.Obstacle Avoidance: Omnidirectional — front, back, left, right, up, down sensing
specs.Vertical Shooting: Native true vertical at full 4K (gimbal rotates 90°)

whatWorks: Omnidirectional obstacle sensing dramatically reduces crash risk — sensors cover all six directions, catching trees, power lines, and structures in real time. Owners who switched from non-sensing drones report a completely different confidence level.

Genuinely cinematic 4K footage with 10-bit D-Log M at this weight — testers who flew across seven countries called it as capable as any travel documentary setup and stopped comparing it to anything more expensive.
Native true vertical shooting at full 4K resolution is a practical differentiator for TikTok and Reels creators — no horizontal-to-vertical crop required.
DJI O4 transmission holds signal reliably at 3+ km in open conditions, and one tester flew over 4 miles without losing a single bar.
worthKnowing: Canadian drone regulations: at 249g the Mini 4 Pro qualifies as a micro RPAS under Transport Canada's CARS, meaning no drone registration and no pilot certificate required for recreational flying. Standard rules still apply — VLOS, below 122m AGL, no restricted airspace, no endangering aviation. No paperwork, but not unregulated.
Real-world flight time runs 28–31 minutes under active 4K recording — not the 34-minute spec. Plan battery count accordingly; the Fly More Combo is worth it.
Wind resistance is the honest limitation — at 249g the Mini 4 Pro handles Level 5 winds (24 mph) but is noticeably affected by gusts that heavier drones like the Air 3S absorb without drifting.
Night mode requires ambient light — true darkness still produces noisy footage. The 1/1.3-inch sensor is excellent for a drone but not magic.

commonComplaints.title: Real flight time is 28–31 minutes…
commonComplaints.body: Real flight time is 28–31 minutes with 4K recording, not 34 minutes as advertised — the spec only holds under ideal conditions

commonComplaints.title: The Fly More Combo bag can't…
commonComplaints.body: The Fly More Combo bag can't fit the RC 2 controller with the visor and neck strap attached — you have to remove the sticks every time

commonComplaints.title: Lightweight design makes it noticeably susceptible…
commonComplaints.body: Lightweight design makes it noticeably susceptible to wind — any gusts above 20 mph and the footage shows drift that a heavier drone wouldn't show

commonComplaints.title: DJI-supplied prop keeper is harder to…
commonComplaints.body: DJI-supplied prop keeper is harder to install than aftermarket options and puts more stress on the blades

verdict: The Mini 4 Pro remains the benchmark sub-250g drone — omnidirectional sensing, 4K/100fps with 10-bit D-Log M, and O4 transmission range in a 249g body that skips Transport Canada registration requirements entirely. Real-world flight time runs short of spec, and wind resistance is the honest ceiling. For travel photographers and content creators, nothing at this weight class competes.

realBuyers.quote: The obstacle avoidance gave me confidence I'd never had with drones before. Flew it through a forest without a single collision.
After logging over 6 hours of airtime across seven countries, if you don't need thermal imaging or rain protection, you don't need anything more expensive than this.
The 4K footage looks incredible. I edited a travel film and people kept asking what camera I used — they were shocked it was a 249g drone.
Night mode takes very good video as long as there's at least a little ambient light. Not magic, but genuinely impressive for a mini drone.

metaDescription: DJI Mini 4 Pro review for Canada: omnidirectional obstacle sensing, 4K/100fps, 20km range, 249g — no Transport Canada RPAS registration required. ~$1,089 CAD.
introText: The DJI Mini 4 Pro is DJI's flagship sub-250g drone, combining omnidirectional obstacle sensing, 4K/100fps with 10-bit D-Log M, and DJI O4 transmission range — in a 249g body that sits below Transport Canada's micro RPAS registration threshold. Available on Amazon.ca (ASIN B0CFF1SJ3P) at around $1,089 CAD.
